elements.title: Ryan Davis
elements.contents: ... that video game journalist Ryan Davis (pictured) was honored in Minecraft's soundtrack?

elements.title: a short story
elements.contents: ... that a short story in The Bewitched Bourgeois was described by critics as both a "comedy of errors" and a parable about the inevitability of death?

elements.title: Yundi
elements.contents: ... that Yundi is both the youngest first‑prize winner and the youngest judge in the history of the International Chopin Piano Competition?

elements.title: The Cure
elements.contents: ... that Olivia Rodrigo's song "The Cure", which shares its name with a goth-rock band, was released on World Goth Day?

elements.title: his 2008 ROM hack
elements.contents: ... that Toby Fox described the development of his 2008 ROM hack as excavating ruins "made of crumbly graham crackers" with "hands made of atom bombs"?

elements.title: Kobe Shimbun
elements.contents: ... that the Kyoto Shimbun helped the Kobe Shimbun after their office was destroyed by an earthquake, one year after the two newspapers signed a mutual-aid agreement?

elements.title: Qojin
elements.contents: ... that Genghis Khan's daughter Qojin got married between 23 and 27, older than usual for a Mongol woman?

elements.title: "an invasion of modernity" on her estate
elements.contents: ... that Massachusetts philanthropist Helen Storrow invited "an invasion of modernity" on her estate?

elements.title: an exoplanet orbiting two stars
elements.contents: ... that a 17-year-old discovered an exoplanet orbiting two stars?
